Title: Arabidopsis acyl-CoA-binding proteins regulate the synthesis of lipid signals

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Facilitates OPDA import into peroxisomes for jasmonate synthesis; reduced expression is associated with lower jasmonate accumulation in atacbp6
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene expression association in mutant background; prior functional knowledge summarized in review
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CTS transports OPDA into peroxisomes for jasmonate biosynthesis. 2. Its reduced expression in atacbp6 correlates with impaired jasmonate production. 3. The review discusses CTS mainly as a downstream transport component linked to AtACBP6 function.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for modulating jasmonate formation, though treated here mainly as contextual downstream component
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a common core component in the summarized AtACBP6-jasmonate relationship, but not a primary emphasis of the review relative to ACBPs.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; highlighted as important within sterol C4-demethylation complex
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Demethylated sterol intermediates leading to functional sterols
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the first C4 demethylation step of cycloartenol in sterol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms signaling sterol intermediates and contributes to functional sterol production
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant development, organ patterning, auxin-related developmental regulation, growth signaling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Membranes; developing seeds and epidermal tissues implicated
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SMO1 knockdown or misregulation together with AtACBP1 alters sterol signaling and causes embryogenesis defects, high-oil low-mucilage seeds, and trichome defects
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Double mutant analysis; gene knockdown/silencing; phenotypic analysis of embryos, seeds, and trichomes; gene expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SMO1 catalyzes the first demethylation of cycloartenol in sterol biosynthesis. 2. Its activity is negatively regulated by AtACBP1 through protein interaction. 3. Misregulation of SMO1 perturbs sterol-derived signaling associated with embryogenesis, seed oil/mucilage balance, and trichome development.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Embryo arrest, proembryo abortion, gametophytic defects, altered seed oil and mucilage, abnormal trichome initiation and branching
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for modifying seed oil and epidermal traits, but manipulation carries severe developmental risks due to embryogenesis defects
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review summarizes SMO1-1 and SMO1-2 studies as interacting forms/isoforms in the Arabidopsis sterol C4-demethylation context.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Structural phospholipid degradation to phosphatidic acid
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point in stress-induced PA generation, as discussed in review
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes phosphatidylcholine to generate phosphatidic acid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ABA responses, seed dormancy, stress signaling, membrane-related adaptation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Membranes during germinative and seedling stages
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated together with ABA/stress-responsive context in AtACBP1 overexpressors
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Associated with ABA or drought responses
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum and/or plasma membrane-associated signaling context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein-protein interaction with AtACBP1; overexpression and mutant phenotypes; metabolite accumulation assays for PA; ABA/drought response assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhanced PLDα1-associated PA production promotes ABA sensitivity, seed dormancy, and stress responses when facilitated by AtACBP1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PLDα1 generates phosphatidic acid from phosphatidylcholine in ABA/stress signaling. 2. AtACBP1 facilitates PLDα1-mediated PA synthesis through interaction with both PLDα1 and its lipid substrate/product. 3. Increased PA accumulation correlates with stronger ABA responses and stress phenotypes.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Greater seed dormancy and reduced germinability under salinity/osmotic stress when PA signaling is enhanced
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Abscisic acid; drought; salinity; osmotic stress
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering ABA-linked stress responses, but elevated activity may impair germination and increase dormancy
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review positions PLDα1 as the enzymatic generator of signaling PA, with AtACBP1 acting as regulator rather than PLDα1 itself being the review's central focus.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated by ABA and drought; stress responsive
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated by ABA, drought, salinity, and osmotic stress contexts
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: ABA; drought; salinity; osmotic stress; cold acclimation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein interaction-mediated regulation; lipid sensing/tethering; promotes PLDα1-dependent PA production; negatively regulates SMO1 activity; may tether AREB1 and RAP2.12 at membranes
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions through protein-protein interactions at ER/PM with PLDα1, SMO1, AREB1, RAP2.12
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Phosphatidic acid synthesis; sterol signal generation; ABA-linked lipid signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Upregulates PLDα1-associated ABA/stress responses; negatively regulates SMO1 activity; indirectly affects GL2 and other HD-ZIP IV genes through sterol signal changes
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Stress adaptation, developmental regulation, membrane stabilization
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Membrane compartments; seeds and developing epidermal tissues implicated
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Critical regulatory node linking phospholipid and sterol signal synthesis via direct interactions with metabolic enzymes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes PA accumulation and ABA sensitivity/dormancy; regulates sterol signaling affecting embryogenesis, seed oil/mucilage traits, and trichome development
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression and mutant analysis; protein-protein interaction assays; lipid-binding studies; metabolite profiling; stress and developmental phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtACBP1 is an ER/PM-localized lipid-binding regulator that modulates multiple lipid signaling pathways. 2. It facilitates PLDα1-mediated phosphatidic acid synthesis in ABA/stress signaling. 3. It negatively regulates SMO1 to influence sterol-derived developmental signals. 4. It may tether AREB1 and RAP2.12 at membranes to modulate stress-responsive transcription.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher dormancy, reduced germination under salinity/osmotic stress, severe developmental defects when sterol pathway is misregulated with SMO1 perturbation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering ABA/stress resilience and lipid signaling, but must balance adverse effects on germination and development
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ABA; drought; salinity; osmotic stress; cold acclimation; hypoxia
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is a review article; AtACBP1 is a major summarized core regulator rather than newly discovered in this paper.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Regulates ABA-mediated reactive oxygen species accumulation and stomatal closure; tethers RAP2.12/RAP2.3 at membranes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: ABA-linked stress signaling connected to lipid signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Misregulates ABA signaling genes in overexpressors
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent regulator of ABA-mediated drought response rather than a direct biosynthetic enzyme
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression promotes stomatal closure, reduced transpiration loss, and higher drought tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression analysis; ABA/drought response phenotyping; gene expression analysis; protein interaction studies
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtACBP2 participates in ABA-mediated drought responses. 2. Overexpression alters ABA signaling genes and promotes reactive oxygen species accumulation, stomatal closure, and drought tolerance. 3. It also interacts with RAP2.12 and RAP2.3 in hypoxia-related signaling.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for drought tolerance improvement through ABA-responsive signaling
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the review summarizes it as a core ACBP isoform regulating lipid-related stress signaling, although a direct terminal metabolite is not emphasized.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Implicated in jasmonate-mediated defense and hypoxia/submergence responses
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Wounding; submergence; ethanol-related hypoxic stress; pathogen challenge
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Influences fatty acid and oxylipin composition in phloem via apoplastic transport; modulates VLC-sphingolipid biosynthesis through ligand binding
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Jasmonate-responsive marker genes; VLCFA-containing sphingolipid pathway
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Jasmonate synthesis/transport; VLC-sphingolipid biosynthesis; salicylate-linked hypoxia signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Required for full induction of jasmonate-responsive marker genes after wounding
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Methyl jasmonate; jasmonates; VLCFA-containing glycosyl inositol phosphoceramides; salicylate-linked signals
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Stress and defense signaling, systemic responses, senescence control
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important isoform-specific regulator connecting jasmonate, sphingolipid, and salicylate signaling through acyl-CoA binding and transport
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of function lowers methyl jasmonate and weakens wound responses but improves tolerance to submergence/ethanol stress; overexpression has opposite effects
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant, RNAi, and overexpression analysis; phloem exudate metabolite profiling; micrografting; stress phenotyping; genetic rescue with npr1-5
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtACBP3 promotes jasmonate-related wound signaling by affecting fatty acid and oxylipin composition in phloem. 2. It regulates VLC-sphingolipid accumulation during hypoxia/submergence responses. 3. Its effects intersect with salicylate/NPR1-dependent signaling and defense/senescence phenotypes.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression causes hypersensitivity to submergence and ethanol stress and accelerates salicylate-dependent senescence
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential leverage point for tuning wound defense and hypoxia responses, but opposing effects on tolerance and senescence require caution
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Wounding; submergence; ethanol stress; bacterial pathogen infection
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Binds both LC- and VLC-acyl-CoA esters, suggesting ligand-specific regulatory roles across jasmonate, sphingolipid, and salicylate signaling.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Promoter activity systemically upregulated by wounding
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Likely facilitates CTS-mediated OPDA transport by influencing precursor/oxylipin pools
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Associated with downregulation of CTS in atacbp6
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of AtACBP6 lowers jasmonates, linoleic acid, and linolenic acid but increases cis-OPDA in phloem exudates
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant metabolite profiling of phloem exudates; promoter activity assays; gene expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtACBP6 is associated with jasmonate biosynthesis in Arabidopsis. 2. atacbp6 has reduced phloem jasmonates and elevated cis-OPDA. 3. Its wound-inducible promoter activity and effect on CTS expression support a role in OPDA transport and oxylipin signaling.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modulate wound-induced jasmonate signaling
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct binding of AtACBP6 to OPDA-CoA had not been verified in the reviewed studies.
